extra.message: User input from request.json['search'] is directly concatenated into a raw SQL query using Python string formatting and executed via db.engine.execute(), enabling SQL injection.

The 404 error handler dynamically constructs a Jinja2 template string using Python string interpolation with request.url, then renders it with render_template_string(). An attacker can inject Jinja2 template syntax into the URL to achieve Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI) and remote code execution.

The search_customer exception handler dynamically constructs a Jinja2 template string with str(e), which includes the user-controlled search term from the failed SQL query. render_template_string() executes any Jinja2 syntax present in the error message, leading to Server-Side Template Injection.
jwt.decode() is called with verify=False, completely disabling signature verification. This allows attackers to forge arbitrary JWT tokens and bypass authentication.
The /get/<cust_id> endpoint uses insecure_verify() which does not verify JWT signatures. Any forged or tampered JWT token will be accepted, allowing authentication bypass.

The /fetch/customer endpoint retrieves any customer record by ID without verifying that the authenticated user is authorized to access that specific record. This allows authenticated users to access other customers' data (IDOR).

cwe: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key

extra.message: The /get/<cust_id> endpoint allows any authenticated user (including those with forged tokens) to retrieve arbitrary customer records by ID without authorization checks, resulting in Insecure Direct Object Reference.

cwe: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key

extra.message: Hardcoded secret keys (SECRET_KEY_HMAC='secret', SECRET_KEY_HMAC_2='am0r3C0mpl3xK3y', secret_key='F12Zr47j\3yX R~X@H!jmM]Lwf/,?KT') are embedded in the source code. Compromise of the source code allows session forgery and token manipulation.
